Final response excerpt:
Construct read the actual codebase and came back with a clear pick: **a dedicated `/map` page** (Option 1). The reasoning, grounded in what's really there:

- **Your homepage is a 968-line machine** — hero, search, fuzzy-match filter script, the whole 78-card grid. A `[List | Map]` toggle would bolt two-view state management onto the one page you can least afford to regress. A map-first hero is worse — it demotes the proven search/grid.
- **Leaflet would be your first client-JS dependency** (the site is 100% static HTML + tiny inline scripts today, zero islands). A `/map` page quarantines that ~45KB payload so it loads *only* when someone visits the map — the homepage stays exactly as fast as it is now.
